abstract: In this 2011 biennial review, the IUPAC Commission on Isotopic Abundances and Atomic Weights has updated the standard atomic weights for five chemical elements based on recent high-precision measurements and evaluations of isotopic variability in nature. Significant revisions include the transition of bromine and magnesium to interval notation—[79.901, 79.907] and [24.304, 24.307], respectively—to better represent the natural variations of their atomic weights in terrestrial materials. Additionally, the standard atomic weights for germanium, indium, and mercury have been refined to $72.630(8)$, $114.818(1)$, and $200.592(3)$. This report details the methodology for determining these intervals and provides updated tables for all elements, including conventional atomic-weight values for practical use in trade and commerce. The report also highlights the development of the "Periodic Table of the Isotopes" as a new educational tool and catalogs the relative atomic masses and half-lives of selected radionuclides.
